req.title: System-Admin views performance-review
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a performance-review through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: This test suite validates that authenticated system-admin users can view performance-reviews via REST API with proper validation, audit logging, and error handling. The suite covers positive (successful view with audit), negative (auth/authz failures, validation errors, module disabled), and boundary (malformed IDs, missing resources) scenarios using equivalence partitioning, boundary value analysis, decision tables, and negative testing heuristics. Each test is independent, deterministic, and traces to specific requirement outcomes.

tests.name: test_system_admin_view_performance_review_success
preconditions: User is authenticated with system-admin role
Performance-review with ID 'pr-001' exists in database
actions: Issue GET request to /api/performance-reviews/pr-001 with valid auth token
expected: Response body contains entity_id 'pr-001' and mandatory fields (id, employee_id, rating, review_date)
Audit record created with action='VIEW_PERFORMANCE_REVIEW' and actor='system-admin'
trace: R-WEB-024: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a performance-review
R-WEB-024: response 200 or 201 with entity id
R-WEB-024: audit record must be written on success
tests.executable:
import requests
import json
from datetime import datetime

def test_system_admin_view_performance_review_success():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8000'
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_admin_token>'}
    
    response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/api/performance-reviews/pr-001',
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 200
    data = response.json()
    assert data['id'] == 'pr-001'
    assert 'employee_id' in data
    assert 'rating' in data
    assert 'review_date' in data
    
    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/api/audit-logs',
        headers=headers,
        params={'action': 'VIEW_PERFORMANCE_REVIEW', 'entity_id': 'pr-001'}
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    audit_logs = audit_resp.json()
    assert len(audit_logs) > 0
    assert audit_logs[0]['actor'] == 'system-admin'

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_view_performance_review_denied
preconditions: User is not authenticated (no valid token provided)
actions: Issue GET request to /api/performance-reviews/pr-001 without auth token
Capture response status and error message
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_view_performance_review_denied():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8000'
    
    response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/api/performance-reviews/pr-001'
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 401
    data = response.json()
    assert 'error' in data or 'message' in data
    assert 'authentication' in data.get('message', '').lower() or \
           'unauthenticated' in data.get('error', '').lower()

tests.name: test_non_admin_user_view_performance_review_forbidden
preconditions: User is authenticated with role 'employee' (not admin)
actions: Issue GET request to /api/performance-reviews/pr-001 with employee auth token
Capture response status and error message
expected: No audit record created with successful view action
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_non_admin_user_view_performance_review_forbidden():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8000'
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_employee_token>'}
    
    response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/api/performance-reviews/pr-001',
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 403
    data = response.json()
    assert 'error' in data or 'message' in data
    assert 'forbidden' in data.get('message', '').lower() or \
           'permission' in data.get('error', '').lower()

tests.name: test_view_performance_review_with_invalid_id_format
preconditions: User is authenticated with system-admin role
Performance-review ID format is invalid (empty string, special chars)
actions: Issue GET request to /api/performance-reviews with malformed ID parameter
Capture response status and validation error
expected: Error message indicates validation failure on ID format
trace: R-WEB-024: Mandatory fields must be validated

tests.name: test_view_nonexistent_performance_review
preconditions: User is authenticated with system-admin role
actions: Capture response status and error message
expected: Error message indicates resource does not exist
trace: R-WEB-024: Boundary condition on missing resources
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_view_nonexistent_performance_review():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8000'
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_admin_token>'}
    
    response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/api/performance-reviews/pr-nonexistent',
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 404
    data = response.json()
    assert 'error' in data or 'message' in data
    assert 'not found' in data.get('message', '').lower() or \
           'not found' in data.get('error', '').lower()

tests.name: test_view_performance_review_module_disabled
preconditions: User is authenticated with system-admin role
actions: Issue GET request to /api/performance-reviews/pr-001 with admin token
Capture response status and error message
expected: HTTP 503 Service Unavailable or 400 Bad Request
Error message indicates module is disabled or unavailable
trace: R-WEB-024: Precondition - performance-review module is enabled
R-WEB-024: Negative test coverage for module disabled state
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_view_performance_review_module_disabled():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8000'
    headers = {'Authorization': 'Bearer <valid_admin_token>'}
    
    response = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/api/performance-reviews/pr-001',
        headers=headers,
        params={'module_state': 'disabled'}
    )
    
    assert response.status_code in [503, 400]
    data = response.json()
    assert 'error' in data or 'message' in data
    assert 'disabled' in data.get('message', '').lower() or \
           'unavailable' in data.get('error', '').lower()
